benchmarks.name: EQ-Bench 3
benchmarks.notes: Uses published chart data aggregate.

benchmarks.name: ARC-AGI-2
benchmarks.notes: Uses ARC-AGI-2 leaderboard JSON.

benchmarks.name: BullshitBench
benchmarks.notes: Uses v2 aggregate JSONL.

benchmarks.name: OSWorld
benchmarks.notes: Uses verified results workbook.

benchmarks.name: FrontierSWE
benchmarks.notes: Generic table extraction.

benchmarks.name: HalluHard
benchmarks.notes: Excludes web-search variants.

benchmarks.name: GDPval-AA
benchmarks.notes: Uses Artificial Analysis GDPval-AA Elo leaderboard.

benchmarks.name: Humanity's Last Exam
benchmarks.notes: Uses Artificial Analysis score leaderboard.

benchmarks.name: CritPt
benchmarks.notes: Uses Artificial Analysis score leaderboard.

benchmarks.name: Terminal-Bench Hard
benchmarks.notes: Uses Artificial Analysis score leaderboard.

benchmarks.name: Vending-Bench 2
benchmarks.notes: Normalizes final money balance against a $63k/year good-strategy target.

benchmarks.name: Kaggle Game Arena
benchmarks.notes: Uses Kaggle's pooled Bradley-Terry leaderboard rating across strategic games.

benchmarks.name: Remote Labor Index
benchmarks.notes: Uses the dashboard.safe.ai RLI automation-rate API.

benchmarks.name: SpatialBench
benchmarks.notes: Uses the published SpatialBench pass@1 leaderboard.

benchmarks.name: SlopCodeBench
benchmarks.notes: Uses the published best-version leaderboard and Iso Solve score.

benchmarks.name: Blueprint-Bench 2
benchmarks.notes: Uses Andon Labs connectivity similarity leaderboard.

benchmarks.name: HiL-Bench
benchmarks.notes: Uses Scale Labs combined Pass@3 with ask_human tool.

benchmarks.name: ProgramBench Extended
benchmarks.notes: Averages embedded per-task behavioral test pass-rate scores rather than headline resolved counts.

benchmarks.name: ContextArena
benchmarks.notes: Uses 8-needle MRCRv2 AUC @1M from ContextArena's public API.

benchmarks.name: GBA Eval
benchmarks.notes: Uses the first-party leaderboard JSON overall emulator score.

benchmarks.name: GSO-Bench
benchmarks.notes: Uses first-party Opt@1 leaderboard JSON with hack-adjusted scores.

benchmarks.name: ExploitBench
benchmarks.notes: Uses the site's generated dataset and matched-seed capability coverage leaderboard.

benchmarks.name: DeepSWE
benchmarks.notes: Uses first-party pass@1 leaderboard JSON.

benchmarks.name: LisanBench
benchmarks.notes: Uses first-party mean path length normalized against the current leaderboard maximum.

benchmarks.name: ProofBench
benchmarks.notes: Uses VALS accuracy and excludes Aristotle because the source reports it uses a separate internal harness.

benchmarks.name: Vibe Code Bench v1.1
benchmarks.notes: Uses VALS accuracy.

capability_index_metadata.minimum_benchmark_rule: floor(benchmarks whose oldest dated model release is on or before this model release / 3), minimum 8

vision.minimum_benchmark_rule: floor(benchmarks whose oldest dated model release is on or before this model release / 3)

methodology.capability_index: Capability scores use Epoch AI's public ECI implementation: a logistic item-response model fitted with nonlinear least squares, ridge regularization, score clipping, and bootstrap confidence intervals. Reasoning/context variants are collapsed to a base model key, and each benchmark uses the highest-scoring variant for that base model. The all-benchmark index requires models to appear in at least eight benchmark units and at least one third of benchmark units whose oldest dated model release is on or before the selected model release date, rounded down to the nearest whole number. Category sub-indices use the same one-third category release-date rule and also require the model to appear in at least four benchmarks anywhere in the full dataset. Non-anchor categories include the anchor benchmark only as scale calibration, not for category coverage eligibility. The ECI anchors are declared in capability_index_metadata.
methodology.elo_normalization: Elo benchmarks keep raw Elo for display and use expected win probability against the highest-Elo model on that leaderboard divided by 0.5 for normalized scores, capped at 1.0.
methodology.metr_time_horizon: METR Time Horizon 1.1 is scraped from METR's public YAML and mapped to dashboard model IDs with explicit aliases. Correlations compare capability index values to log2 of METR's p50 time horizon in minutes for overlapping models.
methodology.model_normalization: Model labels are cleaned and mapped to stable lowercase IDs; labs are inferred from providers or model-name rules, then enriched from exact model metadata matches and exact canonical aliases.
methodology.parameter_enrichment: Parameters come from Epoch's public model metadata when matched, exact sourced metadata entries for open-weight models, and explicit sizes in model names. Active parameters are included only when sourced or explicit in the model name.
methodology.score_normalization: Percent scores are divided by 100; native 0-1 scores are kept as-is; all scores are clipped to [0, 1]. Lower-is-better benchmarks keep the reported score and invert the normalized score.
